anchor.snippet: The restricted holonomy group based at x is the subgroup

annotations.note: No holonomy group exists in Mathlib, hence no restricted holonomy subgroup from contractible loops.

annotations.label: Basepoint dependence up to conjugation

anchor.section: Holonomy of a connection in a vector bundle
anchor.snippet: the holonomy group depends on the basepoint x only up to conjugation

annotations.note: No holonomy group is defined in Mathlib, so this conjugation result is absent.

anchor.section: Holonomy of a connection in a principal bundle
anchor.snippet: The restricted holonomy group based at p is the subgroup

annotations.note: No principal-bundle holonomy group exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Basepoint dependence (principal bundle)

anchor.section: Holonomy of a connection in a principal bundle
anchor.snippet: the holonomy group depends on the basepoint p only up to conjugation in G

annotations.note: Principal-bundle holonomy is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Properties of holonomy and restricted holonomy groups

anchor.section: Holonomy of a connection in a principal bundle
anchor.snippet: Some important properties of the holonomy and restricted holonomy groups include:

annotations.note: These structural properties depend on holonomy groups, which Mathlib does not define.

annotations.label: Holonomy bundle

anchor.snippet: is called the holonomy bundle

annotations.note: No holonomy or holonomy bundle (reduction of structure group) exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Equivariance of holonomy bundle

anchor.snippet: the holonomy bundle also transforms equivariantly

annotations.note: The holonomy bundle is undefined in Mathlib, so its equivariance is not stated.

annotations.label: Monodromy group

anchor.snippet: is called the monodromy group of the connection

annotations.note: Mathlib defines the monodromy action of a covering map (the topological analog) but not the monodromy group of a flat connection.

annotations.label: Monodromy representation

anchor.snippet: is a monodromy representation of the fundamental group

annotations.note: Mathlib's monodromyFunctor gives a fundamental-groupoid representation for covering maps, the topological analog rather than the connection-theoretic monodromy representation.

annotations.label: Local holonomy group

anchor.snippet: The local holonomy group at a point p is defined by

annotations.note: Local holonomy is not formalized since Mathlib has no holonomy group.

annotations.label: Properties of the local holonomy group

anchor.snippet: The local holonomy group has the following properties:

annotations.note: No local holonomy group exists in Mathlib, so these properties are absent.

annotations.label: Local equals restricted holonomy under constant dimension

anchor.snippet: If the dimension of the local holonomy group is constant, then the local and restricted holonomy agree:

annotations.note: Neither local nor restricted holonomy is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Curvature as differential of holonomy

anchor.snippet: the curvature is the differential of the holonomy action at the identity of the holonomy group

annotations.note: Mathlib has no holonomy action, so this curvature-holonomy relation is absent.

annotations.label: Ambrose–Singer theorem

anchor.snippet: is spanned by all the elements of

annotations.note: The Ambrose–Singer theorem relating curvature to the holonomy Lie algebra is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ambrose–Singer theorem (holonomy bundle form)

anchor.snippet: is the subspace of g spanned by elements of the form

annotations.note: No principal-bundle holonomy algebra or Ambrose–Singer statement exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Riemannian holonomy

anchor.snippet: is the holonomy group of the Levi-Civita connection on the tangent bundle

annotations.note: Mathlib has Riemannian metrics but neither the Levi-Civita connection nor its holonomy group.

annotations.label: Generic holonomy is O(n) or SO(n)

anchor.snippet: n - dimensional Riemannian manifold has an O( n ) holonomy

annotations.note: Riemannian holonomy is undefined in Mathlib, so this O(n)/SO(n) claim is absent.

annotations.label: Borel–Lichnerowicz theorem

anchor.snippet: the restricted holonomy group is a closed Lie subgroup of O( n )

annotations.note: The Borel–Lichnerowicz closedness theorem for restricted holonomy is not formalized.

annotations.label: Reducible manifold

anchor.section: Reducible holonomy and the de Rham decomposition
anchor.snippet: M is said to be reducible

annotations.note: Reducibility defined via holonomy action is absent since holonomy is unformalized.

annotations.label: Reducible manifold is locally a product

anchor.section: Reducible holonomy and the de Rham decomposition
anchor.snippet: So M is locally a Cartesian product

annotations.note: Mathlib has no holonomy-based local product splitting of Riemannian manifolds.

annotations.label: de Rham decomposition theorem

anchor.section: Reducible holonomy and the de Rham decomposition
anchor.snippet: Then locally M is isometric to a product

annotations.note: The de Rham local isometric splitting theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Global de Rham decomposition (geodesically complete)

anchor.section: Reducible holonomy and the de Rham decomposition
anchor.snippet: then the theorem holds globally, and each M i is a geodesically complete manifold

annotations.note: The global de Rham decomposition for complete simply connected manifolds is not formalized.

annotations.label: Berger classification

anchor.snippet: Berger gave a complete classification of possible holonomy groups for simply connected, Riemannian manifolds which are irreducible

annotations.note: Berger's classification of irreducible Riemannian holonomy groups is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sp(n)·Sp(1) manifolds carry a parallel 4-form

anchor.snippet: such manifolds would necessarily carry a parallel 4-form

annotations.note: Quaternion-Kähler structures and parallel forms are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: G2/Spin(7) manifolds are Ricci-flat

anchor.snippet: such a manifold would necessarily be Ricci-flat

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Ricci curvature nor G2/Spin(7) holonomy, so this is absent.

annotations.label: Spin(9) holonomy implies locally symmetric

anchor.snippet: to be necessarily locally symmetric

annotations.note: Neither Spin(9) holonomy nor locally symmetric spaces are formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Inclusions of special holonomy manifolds

anchor.snippet: every hyperkähler manifold is a Calabi–Yau manifold

annotations.note: Special holonomy classes (hyperkähler, Calabi–Yau, Kähler) are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Irreducible holonomy acts transitively on unit sphere

anchor.snippet: it acts transitively on the unit sphere

annotations.note: Simon's transitivity theorem for non-symmetric irreducible holonomy is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Holonomy of Riemannian symmetric spaces

anchor.snippet: have local holonomy isomorphic to H

annotations.note: Riemannian symmetric spaces and their holonomy are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Special holonomy and parallel spinors

anchor.snippet: the following facts hold:

annotations.note: Mathlib has no spinor bundles or parallel-spinor correspondence with special holonomy.

annotations.label: Affine holonomy groups

anchor.snippet: Affine holonomy groups are the groups arising as holonomies of torsion-free affine connections

annotations.note: Mathlib has torsion infrastructure for covariant derivatives but no affine holonomy group.

annotations.label: Berger's two criteria

anchor.snippet: two criteria that must be satisfied by the Lie algebra of the holonomy group

annotations.note: Berger's algebraic criteria on holonomy Lie algebras are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hermitian symmetric space affine holonomies

anchor.snippet: then both H and C *· H are non-symmetric irreducible affine holonomy groups

annotations.note: This classification result on affine holonomy groups is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quaternion-Kähler symmetric space affine holonomies

anchor.snippet: then H is a non-symmetric irreducible affine holonomy groups

annotations.note: Affine holonomy groups from quaternion-Kähler symmetric spaces are not formalized in Mathlib.
